metadata.title: FSL Independent Replay Matrix
notes: This matrix is a derived reviewer index, not a new theorem authority.
Lean source, theorem registry, coverage audit, lifecycle export, assumptions inventory, and checksums remain the evidence authorities.
Checksum references are copied from the public package checksum file where available.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Zooming into an HTM cell advances to a deeper spatial resolution.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: A zoomed child cell preserves the parent relation needed for bounded localization.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Face adjacency is symmetric, so local neighbor claims can be checked in either direction.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: A face is not adjacent to itself, ruling out zero-length face moves.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Declared face adjacency matches the expected HTM neighbor relation.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Inner sibling transitions are valid local moves inside the same parent region.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Depth-one adjacency is correctly recognized for the first refinement layer.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: HTM neighbor motion is symmetric at the cell level.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: No HTM cell is its own neighbor, preventing fake movement by self-loop.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Neighbor movement preserves depth, keeping local motion on a consistent resolution layer.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: A governed bounded observer decomposes into identity, position, horizon, and proof-bearing state.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Observer continuity requires DID preservation across admissible state transitions.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: A valid observer move advances the temporal tick.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: A valid movement preserves the observer DID.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: A valid move cannot certify a spatial self-loop as movement.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: A valid history cannot reuse the same movement tick.

records.claim_boundary: HTM combinatorial half-bound at observer depth; not arbitrary physical spherical visibility geometry.
records.summary: Axiom-free HTM root-face partition theorem: visible cells at observer depth are bounded by half of the combinatorial HTM surface.

records.claim_boundary: Product-cycle strict-prefix theorem for a modeled coordination cycle; not a complete temporal phenomenology model.
records.summary: Axiom-free temporal product-cycle theorem: a positive observer window is a strict prefix of a larger coordination cycle.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Any governed observation or movement has positive cost.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Combined observer cost is at least its left component cost.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Combined observer cost is at least its right component cost.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Combined observer cost is bounded below by the maximum component cost.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Bounded observation can leave multiple world states consistent with the same evidence.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Semantic anchors need not be unique under bounded observation.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked under explicit cryptographic/model axioms; not assumption-free and not a concrete runtime cryptography proof.
records.summary: A governed observer cannot equivocate between incompatible commitments under the same identity.

records.claim_boundary: Non-empty dark complement at current HTM depth; not a claim about every possible sensor model.
records.summary: Every governed bounded observer has a non-empty spatial dark complement at its current HTM depth.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Zero-cost observation or movement is impossible under governed bounds.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: An observer cannot fully certify itself without an external proof relation.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: Checked unified bridge: state decomposition, valid-history tick advance, no admissible self-loop, positive coordination cost, and zero-cost impossibility.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: FSL-registered movement history remains valid after an admissible motion step.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: FSL-registered spatial motion must be valid against the HTM neighbor surface.

records.claim_boundary: Machine-checked theorem record within the public Lean/model boundary stated in the formal whitepaper.
records.summary: FSL-registered stay/motion validation preserves the bounded observer state.
